title: paspalum plicatulum var. genuinum
intro: Paspalum plicatulum var. genuinum, also known as Bokhara grass, is a type of grass native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Traditionally, it has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Paspalum plicatulum var. genuinum include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
